drugs.notes: 100mg twice daily (approximately 12 hours apart). Swallow whole with water. May be taken with or without food. Avoid grapefruit, grapefruit juice and Seville oranges. If dose missed by more than 3 hours, skip and take next dose at scheduled time. Available as 100mg capsules. btk_inhibitor_eligible context applies.

dose_modifications.condition: Grade 4 neutropenia or Grade 3 neutropenia with fever OR platelets <50x10^9/L with bleeding OR platelets <25x10^9/L — 1st or 2nd toxicity occurrence
dose_modifications.notes: Hold until recovery to Grade 1 or baseline; restart at original dose (100mg twice daily)

dose_modifications.condition: Grade 4 neutropenia or Grade 3 neutropenia with fever OR platelets <50x10^9/L with bleeding OR platelets <25x10^9/L — 3rd toxicity occurrence
dose_modifications.notes: Hold until recovery to Grade 1 or baseline; restart at reduced dose 100mg once daily

dose_modifications.condition: Grade 4 neutropenia or relevant thrombocytopenia — 4th toxicity occurrence
dose_modifications.notes: Discontinue acalabrutinib permanently
No dose adjustment required
No dose adjustment required
Not recommended

dose_modifications.condition: Renal impairment — eGFR ≥30 ml/min/1.73m² (mild or moderate)
dose_modifications.notes: No dose adjustment required

dose_modifications.condition: Renal impairment — eGFR <29 ml/min/1.73m² or on dialysis
dose_modifications.notes: No data available; prescribe only if benefit outweighs risk; monitor carefully for toxicity
Reduce dose to 100mg once daily
Avoid concomitant use; if short-term (up to 7 days, e.g. antibiotics), interrupt acalabrutinib
Avoid concomitant use; if unavoidable, increase acalabrutinib dose to 200mg twice daily

dose_modifications.condition: Non-haematological NCI-CTC Grade 3 or above toxicity
dose_modifications.notes: Delay until resolved to Grade 1 or below; resume using the dose modification table

pre_medications: Aciclovir 400mg twice daily oral (anti-infective prophylaxis, ongoing)
Co-trimoxazole 960mg once daily on Monday, Wednesday and Friday oral (PCP prophylaxis, ongoing)
Allopurinol 300mg once daily oral for 7 days (cycle 1 only — tumour lysis prophylaxis)
special_warnings: BTK inhibitor — oral chemotherapy NPSA/2008/RRR001 alert applies; must be prescribed under supervision of consultant haematologist or oncologist
Atrial fibrillation risk — ensure adequate cardiac function before and regularly during treatment; baseline ECG for all patients; echocardiogram in patients with cardiac history or elderly
Bleeding risk — withhold at least 3–7 days pre- and post-surgery depending on surgery type and bleeding risk; caution with concomitant anticoagulants or antiplatelet agents
Avoid co-administration with proton pump inhibitors; if acid reduction needed use H2-receptor antagonist or antacid with 2-hour gap from acalabrutinib
Avoid grapefruit, grapefruit juice and Seville oranges
Hepatitis B and C reactivation risk — check status prior to treatment
Many CYP3A drug interactions — always check before prescribing concomitant agents
May increase photosensitivity — advise sun protection
Negative pregnancy test required before starting treatment in women of childbearing potential
Proton pump inhibitor use is an EXCLUSION criterion for this protocol
btk_inhibitor_eligible context: patients must meet all inclusion criteria including CD20+ CLL diagnosis by IWCLL 2008 criteria

drugs.notes: Week 1 Day 1 dose escalation step: 3mg in 100ml NaCl 0.9% over 2 hours. If rigors occur, slow infusion; may extend to 8 hours. Pethidine 12.5–25mg IV under doctor supervision for rigors. Non-vesicant.

Week 1 Day 2 dose escalation step: 10mg in 100ml NaCl 0.9% over 2 hours. If acute moderate-to-severe cytokine release reactions occur at 3mg or 10mg, repeat those doses until well tolerated before escalating further.
Week 1 Day 3: 30mg in 100ml NaCl 0.9% over 2 hours.

Weeks 2–12, Days 1, 3 and 5 of each week (Monday/Wednesday/Friday pattern): 30mg in 100ml NaCl 0.9% over 6 hours. Duration 4–12 weeks total; discontinue at complete response, plateau ≥4 weeks, or disease progression. Available as 30mg vials.

dose_modifications.notes: Refer to responsible consultant; any dose reductions or delays dependent on clinical circumstances and treatment intent. Consider blood transfusion or erythropoietin (NICE TA323) if Hb <8g/dL or symptomatic anaemia.
No studies conducted; dose adjustment is a clinical decision but unlikely to require reduction
No dose adjustment recommended in renal impairment

pre_medications: Chlorphenamine 10mg IV — administer 15–30 minutes prior to each alemtuzumab infusion
Paracetamol 1000mg oral — administer 15–30 minutes prior to each alemtuzumab infusion
Alemtuzumab should be started 30 minutes after pre-medication
If infusion ongoing >4 hours after premedication: repeat chlorphenamine 10mg IV and paracetamol 1000mg oral
If severe infusion-related reaction: hydrocortisone 200mg IV 30 minutes pre-dose
Pethidine 12.5–25mg IV (under doctor supervision) for alemtuzumab-induced rigors
Aciclovir 400mg twice daily oral — for duration of treatment and 3 months after (anti-viral prophylaxis)
Co-trimoxazole 960mg once daily on Monday, Wednesday and Friday oral — for duration of treatment and 3 months after (PCP prophylaxis)
Fluconazole 50mg once daily oral — for duration of treatment only (antifungal prophylaxis)
Allopurinol 300mg once daily oral for 7 days (cycle 1 only — tumour lysis prophylaxis)
Gastric protection (PPI or H2 antagonist) — in patients at high risk of GI ulceration or bleed
special_warnings: Off-licence named patient supply from Clinigen — requires prior authorisation
Irradiated blood products required for life following alemtuzumab treatment
Intense pre-medication requirements mandatory before every infusion
PCP prophylaxis (co-trimoxazole) and antiviral prophylaxis (aciclovir) mandatory during and for 3 months after treatment
Weekly CMV PCR monitoring required throughout treatment

Pre-treatment screening mandatory: CMV serology and PCR, hepatitis B core antibody and surface Ag, hepatitis C antibody, EBV, VZV, HIV 1+2 (after consent), TP53 mutation analysis, FBC, FISH for TCL1 and TCRAD gene rearrangements, karyotype, biochemistry, glucose

Cytokine release syndrome risk — infusion-related reactions including hypotension, rigors, fever, shortness of breath, chills, rashes and bronchospasm; infusion may be extended to 8 hours if rigors occur
Tumour lysis risk — pre-hydrate with 1 litre NaCl 0.9% over 4–6 hours in patients with bulk disease; assess TLS risk pre-treatment
Minimal emetic risk
Discontinue if complete response achieved, plateau for ≥4 weeks without further improvement, or disease progression
Urine pregnancy test required before cycle 1 in women of childbearing potential
Written consent and MDT confirmation required before treatment

drugs.notes: Administered TWICE daily at 12-hour intervals (10:00 and 22:00) on days 1, 3 and 5 (6 doses in total per cycle). Each dose: 3000mg/m² in 250ml NaCl 0.9% over 240 minutes at 63ml/hour. Each infusion bag contains TWO doses (500ml total). Administered via CADD Solis VIP pump. NaCl 0.9% 100ml continuous infusion at 0.5ml/hr via Y-site (days 1–6) to maintain line patency. Dose-banded at 100mg/ml. AMBULATORY CARE pathway only. Dose may be decreased to 1500mg/m² in patients >60 years or relapsed post-transplant. Total cycle dose: 18000mg/m².

dose_modifications.notes: Reduce to 50% of original dose; escalate in subsequent cycles if no toxicity
Reduce to 60% of original dose
Reduce to 50% of original dose
Discuss with consultant before prescribing

In general, treatment can proceed if neutrophils >1x10^9/L and platelets >100x10^9/L; always check with consultant. Cycle 2 only proceeds after neutrophil and platelet recovery and response assessment.

pre_medications: Ondansetron 8mg oral or IV on days 1, 3, 5 (starting 15–30 minutes prior to chemotherapy)
Metoclopramide 10mg oral or IV on days 1, 3, 5 (starting 15–30 minutes prior to chemotherapy)
Ondansetron 8mg oral once daily in evening day 1, then 8mg twice daily for 6 days (take home)
Metoclopramide 10mg oral up to twice daily (afternoon and evening) on day 1, then three times daily as required (take home)
Aciclovir 400mg oral three times daily for 28 days (take home — until neutrophils >1x10^9/L)
Prednisolone 0.5% eye drops — instil 1 drop into both eyes four times daily for 10 days (take home — ocular prophylaxis)
Allopurinol 300mg oral once daily for 7 days (cycle 1/induction only; review for consolidation)
Nystatin 1ml oral four times daily (take home — antifungal mouth rinse)
Sodium Chloride 0.9% oral rinse 10ml four times daily as needed (take home)
Chlorhexidine gluconate 10ml mouthwash four times daily as needed (take home — mucositis)
Gastric protection (PPI or H2 antagonist) — if high risk of GI ulceration or bleed (take home)
Ciprofloxacin 250mg twice daily oral — commence only when directed by haematology team if neutrophils <0.5x10^9/L (take home)
Posaconazole 300mg once daily oral — commence only when directed by haematology team if neutrophils <0.5x10^9/L (take home)
special_warnings: AMBULATORY CARE pathway only — NOT for inpatient use under this protocol code
Cytarabine is TWICE daily — TWO doses delivered in one infusion bag via CADD pump; risk of dosing error
Standard dose 3000mg/m²; may be reduced to 1500mg/m² in patients aged >60 years or relapsed post-transplant where 3000mg/m² may not be tolerated
Cerebellar toxicity risk at high-dose cytarabine — monitor for dizziness, confusion, headache
Ocular toxicity — prednisolone eye drops mandatory throughout and 5 days after cytarabine
If third dose reduction is necessary, treatment should be stopped
All dose reductions should not be re-escalated in subsequent cycles without consultant approval
Antifungal choice should be discussed with consultant

drugs.notes: Administered TWICE daily at 12-hour intervals (17:00 and 05:00) on days 1, 3 and 5 (6 doses in total per cycle). Each dose: 1500mg/m² in 120ml NaCl 0.9% over 240 minutes at 30ml/hour. Each CADD cassette contains TWO doses (250ml total). Administered via CADD Solis VIP pump. NaCl 0.9% 100ml continuous infusion at 0.5ml/hr via Y-site (days 1–6) to maintain line patency. Dose-banded at 100mg/ml. AMBULATORY CARE pathway only. Patients >60 years should be considered for intermediate dose cytarabine at 1000mg/m² (days 1–5 per AML 18). Total cycle dose: 9000mg/m².

dose_modifications.notes: Reduce to 50% of original dose; escalate in subsequent cycles if no toxicity

Prednisolone 0.5% eye drops — instil 1 drop into both eyes four times daily for 10 days (take home — ocular prophylaxis)

Chlorhexidine gluconate 10ml mouthwash four times daily as needed (mucositis)
Gastric protection (PPI or H2 antagonist) — if high risk of GI ulceration or bleed
Ciprofloxacin 250mg twice daily oral — only when directed by haematology team if neutrophils <0.5x10^9/L

Antifungal — discuss need and choice with consultant

protocol_name: Acute Myeloid Leukaemia - Amsacrine Cytarabine Etoposide (MACE)

drugs.notes: 100mg/m² in 500ml glucose 5% over 60 minutes. MUST NOT be infused in NaCl 0.9% (precipitation/flocculation). Flush line with glucose 5% before and after. Vesicant — handle with care. Dose-banded at 5mg/ml.
200mg/m² in 1000ml NaCl 0.9% over 22 hours. Neutral (irritant in large volumes). Dose-banded at 20mg/ml.
100mg/m² in 500ml NaCl 0.9% over 60 minutes. Irritant. Dose-banded per agreed bands. Anaphylactic reaction risk.

dose_modifications.notes: Reduce to 60% of original dose
Reduce to 50% of original dose; escalate in subsequent cycles if no toxicity

dose_modifications.condition: Hepatic impairment — bilirubin 26–51 µmol/L AND AST/ALT 60–180 units/L
dose_modifications.notes: Reduce to 50% of original dose

dose_modifications.condition: Hepatic impairment — bilirubin >51 µmol/L AND AST/ALT >180 units/L
dose_modifications.notes: Clinical decision — discuss with consultant
Reduce to 75% of original dose

pre_medications: Ondansetron 8mg twice daily oral or IV for 7 days (days 1–7, starting 15–30 minutes prior to chemotherapy)
Metoclopramide 10mg three times daily as required oral or IV for nausea
Aciclovir 400mg twice daily oral (anti-viral prophylaxis until neutrophils >1x10^9/L)
Antifungal prophylaxis according to consultant choice (until neutrophils >1x10^9/L)
Mouthwashes according to local/national mucositis policy
Gastric protection (PPI or H2 antagonist) — in patients at high risk of GI ulceration or bleed
special_warnings: INPATIENT regimen only — all supportive medications must be prescribed on inpatient chart or electronic prescribing system
Cardiac monitoring required — QTc prolongation risk with amsacrine; baseline ECG/ECHO/MUGA scan should be considered in patients with cardiac history, elderly patients, or prior cardiac disease
Hypokalaemia risk with amsacrine — risk of arrhythmias is increased by hypokalaemia; monitor and correct electrolytes
Amsacrine MUST NOT be diluted in NaCl 0.9% — precipitation occurs; use glucose 5% only; flush line with glucose 5% before and after
Amsacrine is a VESICANT — appropriate precautions required
Etoposide anaphylactic reaction risk — have resuscitation equipment available
Cerebellar toxicity risk from cytarabine — monitor for confusion, dizziness, headache
Third dose reduction should result in stopping treatment; dose reductions should not be re-escalated without consultant approval

drugs.notes: 80mg once daily OR 40mg twice daily (approximately 12-hour intervals) — both schedules are acceptable. Must be taken on an empty stomach: avoid food for at least 2 hours before and 1 hour after. Swallow whole with water; do not break, crush or chew. Available as 20mg and 40mg film-coated tablets. If once-daily dose missed by >12 hours, skip. If twice-daily dose missed by >6 hours, skip. T315I mutation negative patients only.

dose_modifications.notes: Withhold until ANC ≥1x10^9/L; if resolved within 2 weeks, resume at starting dose (80mg OD or 40mg BD)

dose_modifications.condition: Neutrophils <1x10^9/L — resolved after more than 2 weeks
dose_modifications.notes: Withhold until ANC ≥1x10^9/L; if resolved after >2 weeks, resume at reduced dose (80mg OD → 40mg OD; 40mg BD → 20mg BD)
Withhold until ANC ≥1x10^9/L then resume at reduced dose
Withhold until platelets ≥50x10^9/L; if resolved within 2 weeks, resume at starting dose

dose_modifications.condition: Platelets <50x10^9/L — resolved after more than 2 weeks
dose_modifications.notes: Withhold until platelets ≥50x10^9/L; if resolved after >2 weeks, resume at reduced dose
Withhold until platelets ≥50x10^9/L then resume at reduced dose

dose_modifications.condition: Hepatic impairment — mild, moderate or severe
dose_modifications.notes: No dose adjustment required; caution and monitoring advised in moderate and severe impairment (no data available)

dose_modifications.condition: Renal impairment — mild, moderate or severe
dose_modifications.notes: No dose adjustment required

Withhold until resolved to <1.5xULN; resume at reduced dose. If reoccurs at reduced dose, permanently discontinue. If unresolved, permanently discontinue and perform diagnostic tests to exclude pancreatitis.

dose_modifications.condition: Non-haematological adverse reaction Grade 3 or higher
dose_modifications.notes: Withhold until resolved to Grade 1 or lower; resume at reduced dose. If not resolved, permanently discontinue.

special_warnings: BCR-ABL T315I mutation test must be performed before starting; this protocol is for T315I-NEGATIVE patients only (see separate protocol for T315I-positive patients)
Patient must have received 2 or more prior TKIs for CML
Oral chemotherapy NPSA/2008/RRR001 alert applies; must be prescribed under supervision of consultant haematologist only
QTc prolongation risk — baseline ECG required for all patients; patients with prolonged QTc or high risk require regular ECG surveillance; monitor magnesium and potassium
Pancreatitis risk — monitor amylase and lipase at baseline, monthly for 3 months, then as clinically indicated; more frequent monitoring in patients with prior pancreatitis
Hypertension — monitor blood pressure at baseline and monthly
Take on an empty stomach — no food for ≥2 hours before and ≥1 hour after dose
Pleural effusion risk
Hepatitis B, C and HIV status must be checked prior to starting; carriers of hepatitis B or C or active disease should be discussed with hepatologist
Annual BNP monitoring
Many drug interactions — always check before co-prescribing
Haematological toxicity typically presents within 8 weeks of starting

protocol_name: Acute Myeloid Leukaemia - Oral Azacitidine (CC-486) Maintenance

drugs.notes: 300mg once daily days 1–14 of each 28-day cycle. Swallow whole with glass of water at same time each day; do not split, crush, dissolve or chew. Can be taken with or without food. If dose missed and it is still the same day, take as soon as possible; do not take two doses on the same day. If dose vomited, do not repeat on same day. Available as 200mg and 300mg film-coated tablets. Schedule may be extended to days 1–21 (maximum 21 days per 28-day cycle) if disease relapse with blast count 5–15%. Discontinue if >15% blasts or unacceptable toxicity. ORAL FORMULATION — NOT interchangeable with injectable azacitidine (SC). Maintenance therapy only; not for treatment of active disease.

dose_modifications.condition: Grade 4 neutropenia or Grade 3 neutropenia with fever — first occurrence
dose_modifications.notes: Interrupt azacitidine; resume at same dose (300mg) once neutrophils return to Grade 2 or lower; consider GCSF

dose_modifications.condition: Grade 4 neutropenia or Grade 3 neutropenia with fever — 2 consecutive cycles
dose_modifications.notes: Interrupt; resume at reduced dose of 200mg once neutrophils return to Grade 2 or lower; consider GCSF. If toxicity persists after dose reduction, reduce treatment duration by 7 days. If toxicity continues after dose and schedule reduction, discontinue.

dose_modifications.condition: Grade 4 thrombocytopenia or Grade 3 thrombocytopenia with bleeding — first occurrence
dose_modifications.notes: Interrupt; resume at same dose (300mg) once platelets return to Grade 2 or lower

dose_modifications.condition: Grade 4 thrombocytopenia or Grade 3 thrombocytopenia with bleeding — 2 consecutive cycles
dose_modifications.notes: Interrupt; resume at reduced dose of 200mg once platelets return to Grade 2 or lower. If toxicity persists, reduce duration by 7 days. If toxicity continues, discontinue.

dose_modifications.condition: Hepatic impairment — mild (BIL ≤ULN with AST >ULN, or BIL 1–1.5×ULN)
dose_modifications.notes: No dose adjustment required
No initial dose adjustment; monitor more frequently for adverse reactions and adjust dose as appropriate

dose_modifications.condition: Renal impairment — mild, moderate or severe
dose_modifications.notes: No initial dose adjustment required

dose_modifications.condition: Grade 3 or higher nausea, vomiting or diarrhoea — first occurrence
dose_modifications.notes: Interrupt; resume at same dose once toxicity resolves to Grade 1 or lower; use supportive antiemetics/antidiarrhoeal

dose_modifications.condition: Grade 3 or higher nausea, vomiting or diarrhoea — re-occurrence
dose_modifications.notes: Interrupt; resume at 200mg once resolved to Grade 1 or lower. If persists after reduction, reduce duration by 7 days. If continues, discontinue.

dose_modifications.condition: Other Grade 3 or higher non-haematological toxicity — first occurrence
dose_modifications.notes: Interrupt; resume at same dose once resolved to Grade 1 or lower

dose_modifications.condition: Other Grade 3 or higher non-haematological toxicity — re-occurrence
dose_modifications.notes: Interrupt until Grade 1 or lower; reduce to 200mg. If persists, reduce duration by 7 days. If continues, discontinue.

pre_medications: Ondansetron 8mg twice daily oral for 14 days (taken 15–30 minutes prior to azacitidine; a further dose can be taken 12 hours later)
Metoclopramide 10mg three times daily as required oral (take home)
Aciclovir 400mg twice daily oral for 28 days (anti-viral prophylaxis)
Senna 15mg at night as required oral (for constipation)
special_warnings: ORAL FORMULATION (CC-486 / Onureg) — NOT interchangeable with injectable subcutaneous azacitidine (Vidaza); different pharmacokinetics and indication
NPSA oral chemotherapy alert (NPSA/2008/RRR001) applies — must be prescribed under supervision of consultant haematologist or oncologist
Maintenance therapy only in AML patients in CR or CRi following intensive cytarabine-based induction who are not proceeding to HSCT
ECOG performance status 0–3 required
Maximum 14 days per cycle; may extend to maximum 21 days per cycle only if disease relapse with blast count 5–15%
Discontinue if >15% blasts or unacceptable toxicity
FBC monitoring every other week for first 2 cycles, then monthly
Many drug interactions — check before initiating treatment
Minor influence on driving/use of machines — fatigue reported; caution advised

protocol_name: Myelodysplastic Syndrome / AML / CMML - Azacitidine SC (5+2 days)

drugs.notes: 75mg/m² subcutaneous injection in water for injections over 1 minute on days 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 8 and 9 (5 days on, 2 days off [weekend], 2 days on = 7 treatment days; unlicensed 5+2 days schedule). Day 1 of each cycle MUST be a Monday. Dose-banded at 25mg/ml. Resuspend syringe before administration: invert 2–3 times then vigorously roll between palms for 30 seconds. Inject into upper arm, thigh or abdomen; rotate sites; new injection at least 2.5cm from previous site; avoid tender, bruised, red or hardened areas. Doses >100mg (4mL) must be split into two separate sites. Minimum 6 cycles; continue until disease progression or intolerance.

dose_modifications.condition: Haematological — nadir ANC ≤1x10^9/L or platelets ≤50x10^9/L (normal baseline counts: ANC >1.5x10^9/L, platelets >75x10^9/L) — recovery NOT achieved within 14 days
dose_modifications.notes: Delay until recovery then use 50% of dose

dose_modifications.condition: Haematological — nadir ANC >1x10^9/L or platelets >50x10^9/L (normal baseline) — recovery NOT achieved within 14 days
dose_modifications.notes: Delay until recovery then use 100% dose

dose_modifications.condition: Haematological — recovery achieved within 14 days (normal baseline counts)
dose_modifications.notes: No dose adjustment necessary if recovery achieved within 14 days

dose_modifications.condition: Haematological — low baseline (ANC <1.5x10^9/L, platelets <75x10^9/L): <50% reduction from baseline at nadir
dose_modifications.notes: Treat on time with no dose reduction

dose_modifications.condition: Haematological — low baseline: >50% reduction from baseline AND improvement in any cell line
dose_modifications.notes: Treat on time with no dose reduction

dose_modifications.condition: Haematological — low baseline: >50% reduction from baseline, NO improvement in any cell line, recovery within 14 days
dose_modifications.notes: Delay treatment until recovery; re-treat without dose reduction

dose_modifications.condition: Haematological — low baseline: >50% reduction from baseline, NO improvement in any cell line, NO recovery within 14 days — bone marrow cellularity 15–50%, recovery ≤21 days
dose_modifications.notes: 100% dose

dose_modifications.condition: Haematological — low baseline: >50% reduction from baseline, NO improvement in any cell line, NO recovery within 14 days — bone marrow cellularity 15–50%, recovery >21 days
dose_modifications.notes: 50% dose

dose_modifications.condition: Haematological — low baseline: >50% reduction from baseline, NO improvement in any cell line, NO recovery within 14 days — bone marrow cellularity <15%, recovery ≤21 days
dose_modifications.notes: 100% dose

dose_modifications.condition: Haematological — low baseline: >50% reduction from baseline, NO improvement in any cell line, NO recovery within 14 days — bone marrow cellularity <15%, recovery >21 days
dose_modifications.notes: 33% dose
Delay until returns to normal or baseline, then resume at 50% dose
Replace with oral sodium bicarbonate; reduce azacitidine dose by 50% at next cycle
No starting dose modification; monitor closely for adverse events. Subsequent modifications based on haematological parameters. Contraindicated in advanced malignant hepatic tumours.

pre_medications: Ondansetron 8mg oral or IV — day 1 (starting 15–30 minutes prior to azacitidine)
Ondansetron 8mg oral or IV once daily — days 2, 3, 4, 5, 8, 9 (take home)
Metoclopramide 10mg three times daily as required oral (take home — for nausea)
Allopurinol 300mg oral once daily for 7 days (cycle 1 only — tumour lysis prophylaxis)
Hydrocortisone 1% cream applied sparingly to injection site up to four times daily as required (cycle 1 only — injection site inflammation)
Senna 15mg oral at night as required (for constipation)
special_warnings: NOT a licensed dosage schedule — the 5+2 days schedule (days 1–5 and 8–9) is unlicensed; prescribe with caution
Day 1 of each cycle MUST be a Monday to allow the 2-day weekend break on days 6–7
Subcutaneous formulation (Vidaza) — NOT interchangeable with oral azacitidine (CC-486/Onureg); different indication and pharmacokinetics
Minimum 6 cycles required before assessing response; continue until disease progression or intolerance
First cycle should be commenced irrespective of haematological parameters
TLS risk assessment required before starting; appropriate TLS prophylaxis based on individual risk
Azacitidine is contraindicated in patients allergic to mannitol
Azacitidine is contraindicated in advanced malignant hepatic tumours
Injection site reactions common — distinguish from soft tissue infections (cellulitis, necrotising fasciitis in rare cases)
Serious hypersensitivity/anaphylactic reactions reported — discontinue immediately and initiate symptomatic treatment
Patients with cardiovascular or pulmonary disease should undergo full cardiopulmonary assessment before and during treatment
Monitor serum bicarbonate, urea and creatinine — severe renal tubular dysfunction can occur (hypophosphataemia, hypokalaemia, hyponatraemia)
FBC weekly during first cycle; LFTs, U&Es and serum bicarbonate at baseline and before each subsequent cycle
